_comment: VERDICT/v1 conformance vectors. Any language port (Python/TS/Go) MUST reproduce content_hash byte-identically by: (1) JCS-canonicalizing `receipt` per RFC 8785, (2) BLAKE3-256 of the canonical UTF-8 bytes, (3) lowercase hex. This is what makes third-party verification possible and is the published proof that the format is implementation-independent. minimal_approve vector is REAL (generated 2026-06-07, verified via /api/verify). council_consensus_deny vector is also REAL: its Ed25519 signature round-trips verify=true (confirmed 2026-06-09 by verdict-crypto/tests/conformance.rs on MBP). NOTE: JCS canonicalization is LIVE in this crate (hash_payload uses serde_jcs::to_string, lib.rs); the content_hash recompute (JCS->BLAKE3) passes locally — content_hash_recompute_matches_golden is green on MBP. The earlier 'non-canonical serde_json / re-verify on NOMAD' caveat is obsolete since the serde_jcs cutover.
signature: ed25519 over the content_hash bytes, lowercase hex (128 chars)

vectors.name: minimal_approve
vectors._note: Golden values generated 2026-06-07 via @noble/ed25519 + @noble/hashes/blake3 on NOMAD. Verified round-trip via POST /api/verify → {valid:true, hash_ok:true, sig_ok:true}.

vectors.name: council_consensus_deny
vectors.expected_jcs: {"actor":{"id":"council","kind":"service"},"decision":{"consensus":{"consensus_zones":["unsafe deletion of shared state"],"dissent_summary":"1 of 5 models favored defer-with-preview","divergence_points":["whether a dry-run would suffice"],"method":"confidence_weighted","model_count":5},"outcome":"deny","rationale_hash":"3333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333"},"decision_id":"01J0000000000000000000000B","inputs_hash":"2222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222","metadata":{"tier":"Mayor"},"policy_id":"high-risk-gate-v2","timestamp":"2026-06-04T15:31:00Z","version":"verdict/v1"}
vectors._note: Golden values generated 2026-06-07 via @noble/ed25519 + @noble/hashes/blake3 on NOMAD. Verified round-trip via POST /api/verify → {valid:true, hash_ok:true, sig_ok:true}. Note: signature is over UTF-8 bytes of the content_hash hex string (matches verifier protocol).

vectors.name: tamper_detection_negative
vectors._comment: Same as minimal_approve but outcome flipped approve->deny WITHOUT re-signing. A conforming verifier MUST return valid=false, hash_ok=false (recomputed hash != stored content_hash). This proves payload-tamper detection, not just signature checking.

vectors.name: adv_unicode_non_ascii
vectors.expected_jcs: {"accents":"àéîõü çğş","cjk":"日本語のテスト","mixed":"café ☕ naïve résumé","unicode":"café"}
vectors._note: Adversarial canonicalization vector (added 2026-07-03). Unsigned: no signature/pubkey — conforming harnesses assert expected_jcs and expected_content_hash only. Expected values generated by the fixed Python verdict.v1 and cross-checked byte-identical against the varp-sdk TS jcsStringify leg.

vectors.name: adv_key_sort_utf16_code_units

receipt.😀: astral emoji (sorts before U+FB33 by UTF-16 units)
receipt.דּ: hebrew dalet dagesh (BMP, above surrogate range)

vectors.expected_jcs: {"1":"digit one","10":"digit ten","a":"ascii","é":"latin e acute","€":"euro sign","😀":"astral emoji (sorts before U+FB33 by UTF-16 units)","דּ":"hebrew dalet dagesh (BMP, above surrogate range)"}

harness_note: Recommended: a `cargo test conformance` that loads this file, for each vector recomputes JCS+blake3 and asserts == expected_content_hash, verifies the signature, and for the negative vector asserts valid==false. Mirror the same test in a Python and a TS package to prove cross-language parity — that parity IS the sellable claim.
_adversarial_note: adv_* vectors (2026-07-03) pin the RFC 8785 edge cases that the original 3 all-ASCII vectors were blind to: non-ASCII literals (ensure_ascii divergence), non-BMP/surrogate pairs, ECMAScript number formatting (1e-7 not 1e-07, integral floats without '.0', -0 -> 0, denormals, e21 boundary), JSON.stringify escape parity, UTF-16 code-unit key sorting, deep nesting and empty containers. They are unsigned; harnesses must assert expected_jcs + expected_content_hash and skip signature checks for vectors without a 'signature' field.
